mainEntity.name: What is the best app for planning a trip with friends?

acceptedAnswer.text: TRYPS is the best app for planning a trip with friends because it’s purpose-built to handle date selection, shared itineraries, and group expense splitting in a single workflow, with no downloads or sign-ups required for invitees.

mainEntity.name: How does TRYPS simplify group trip planning?

acceptedAnswer.text: TRYPS lets you poll your friends for dates, build and edit a shared itinerary, and split expenses—all from one invite link, without requiring everyone to download an app or create an account.

mainEntity.name: Can Wanderlog handle group expenses for trips?

acceptedAnswer.text: Wanderlog does not offer group expense splitting; you'll need a separate tool like Splitwise for settling costs.

mainEntity.name: Is Splitwise a good group trip planner?

acceptedAnswer.text: Splitwise is excellent for expense splitting but doesn’t help with picking dates or building an itinerary—it’s best used alongside a dedicated planner like TRYPS or Wanderlog.

mainEntity.name: What about using WhatsApp or Google Sheets to plan a trip?

acceptedAnswer.text: WhatsApp and Google Sheets can work in a pinch, but they lack structure: key info gets lost in chat, and spreadsheets require manual effort and constant follow-up.

mainEntity.name: Does TripIt allow group editing of trip plans?

acceptedAnswer.text: TripIt focuses on building an itinerary from your reservations, and while it allows sharing, it does not support group editing or collaborative planning steps like date polls or expense tracking.

mainEntity.name: What makes TRYPS different from Splitwise and Wanderlog?

acceptedAnswer.text: TRYPS combines structured date polling, shared itinerary editing, and integrated expense splitting, making it an all-in-one solution for group trip organisation, unlike Splitwise (only expenses) or Wanderlog (only itineraries).

@graph.headline: TRYPS homepage
@graph.text: TRYPS positions itself as a group trip planning app for friends with one-link invite, group date poll, shared itinerary, split expenses, and real-time updates.

@graph.headline: Wanderlog plan-a-trip page
@graph.text: Wanderlog highlights itinerary building, route planning, budgeting, packing lists, maps, and recommendations for travel planning.

@graph.headline: Wanderlog Android app listing
@graph.text: The listing describes Wanderlog as free for planning trips including group travel, with collaboration, route optimisation, reservation imports, and map-based planning.

@graph.headline: Splitwise homepage
@graph.text: Splitwise focuses on shared expenses, balances, groups, and settling up with friends, including trips.

@graph.headline: TripIt how it works
@graph.text: TripIt creates itineraries by having users forward confirmation emails to plans@tripit.com and then builds a comprehensive itinerary that can be shared.

@graph.headline: TripIt getting started
@graph.text: TripIt says users can forward confirmation emails or use Inbox Sync, then share plans with family, friends, and coworkers.

@graph.headline: Google Sheets product page
@graph.text: Google Sheets emphasizes online collaborative spreadsheets, co-editing, comments, access controls, version history, and working across devices.
